title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Superintendent Batter explains the resolution of the mystery to Eileen Bondel. He reveals that Jimmy Sessage poisoned Gerry Wade’s whiskey and soda before Wade went to sleep, which caused the fatigue Wade mentioned in a letter to Miss Lorena Wade. Cezar administered the lethal poison and later planted a bottle, a cup, and an empty chloroform bottle near the unconscious Wade to create confusion or frame him. While Ronnie DeVille eventually deduced that Cezar was the thief, he was betrayed by Lorena Wade, who provided information to Cecil because of her love for him. DeVille was subsequently eliminated while attempting to use the seven alarm clocks to signal that Cecil was his killer.

Batter further details a complex deception orchestrated by Cecil during an incident at the Two-Footed Dragon Church. Cecil staged a struggle to cover his theft of documents. Rather than actually fainting from a gunshot, Cecil climbed down ivy to Mr. O'Roke's room, where O'Roke had been drugged with sleeping pills. Cecil stole the documents and threw them to an accomplice. To create an alibi, Cecil staged a fight, fired his Colt automatic pistol at an imaginary attacker, and then shot his own right arm with a Mauser pistol to simulate being attacked. He then threw the Mauser out the window and destroyed a tooth-marked glove in the fireplace. Batter realized the truth after finding the burnt glove and noting that the gun fell a distance inconsistent with a left-handed throw. He concluded that Lorena Wade’s presence at the terrace was not a coincidence, but that she was the intended recipient of the stolen documents.
